what_it_does: Catalogues reusable UI/design components extracted from multiple pitch-deck and landing-page sources, giving each component a name, visual/behavioural description, prescriptive reuse note, and originating source slug.
observations: The file contains 110 component records drawn from eight source groups: capital-raise, company-info, deck1-2026, pitch-deck, presentation-15p, product-vi-landing, product-vt, and recent-pitches.
Each record follows a consistent four-field schema: name, description, reuse_note, and _src.
Several component names recur across sources with different descriptions and constraints, e.g., StatCard, PersonCard, NetworkGraphDiagram.
Many entries encode hard layout rules: maximum counts per slide, mandatory placement, pairing constraints, and colour semantics.
The visual vocabulary is tightly consistent: warm cream, gold/caramel, charcoal/near-black, soft shadows, dashed gold lines, and diagonal hatch textures.
Branded geometry is explicitly reserved: hexagon for Property Wizard, diamond for Vi, golden circle with V for the universal slide signature.
Some entries are full layout templates rather than single widgets, such as MarketVerticalSlide, Compare column layout, and SymmetricTwoPanel.
Naming conventions are inconsistent: PascalCase, sentence-case, and kebab-case all appear.
standouts: The V brand badge is declared mandatory on every content slide without exception.
The hexagon/diamond product badge pair is brand-identity-critical and must only represent Property Wizard and Vi.
Reuse notes function as design contracts, specifying when a component must, must not, or always appears.
Duplicate component names across sources carry materially different specs, signalling source-specific variants rather than a single canonical definition.
conventions: Records use a fixed four-field object shape.
Descriptions specify dimensions, colours, typography, and spatial relationships.
Reuse notes state constraints imperatively with must/never/always language.
Source provenance is captured only through the _src slug.
Visual components are described as atomic units that compose into rows, grids, bands, or slides.
concerns: Duplicate component names with divergent descriptions could complicate automated merging or symbol matching.
Some dimensional values are approximate ranges rather than exact tokens.
Inconsistent casing and spacing in component names may hinder lookup or deduplication.
There are no version, date, or author fields; provenance is limited to the source slug.
notes: A design-component ledger that captures both visual specs and prescriptive reuse rules across multiple deck and landing-page substrates.
questions: Are duplicate component names intended to represent the same component with source-specific variants, or separate components?
Should the components be normalized into a canonical taxonomy before fusion?
Are the pixel dimensions relative to a fixed slide or page canvas size?
Does _src indicate the origin deck/page or the most recent observed usage?
apparent_themes: Atomic reusable components governed by strict contextual rules
Warm gold/cream brand vocabulary with consistent texture and geometry
Before/after transformation as a core rhetorical pattern
Mandatory brand signature and reserved geometric identity
Slide-layout grammar expressed through bands, rows, panels, and footers
intention_signals: To build a shared, source-agnostic pattern library for pitch and landing-page designs so future compositions can reuse approved visual atoms while respecting hard constraints about placement, pairing, and semantic meaning.
novelty: The reuse_note field acts as a design-contract, recording not just what a component looks like but when, where, and how it must or must not be used, turning visual components into rule-governed symbols.
contribution: Provides a structured inventory of visual components and their contextual constraints, which a fused system can use to recognize recurring patterns, preserve source-specific variants, and enforce design-grammar rules during composition.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON catalog of reusable UI/design components from pitch decks and landing pages, each with a name, visual description, reuse constraints, and source attribution.
intention_for_embedding: To capture and normalize recurring visual components and their usage rules across multiple presentation and landing-page substrates for reuse in a fused design system.

symbols.does: Cream rounded rectangle displaying a prominent gold statistic with a dark sub-label, used in equal-width sibling rows.
symbols.description: The card acts as the fundamental numeric evidence atom for percentages, dollar values, counts, and multipliers. It is intentionally identical at all depth levels and can stand alone when a drop-shadow variant is applied.

symbols.does: White card with gold border and drop shadow built to present a single oversized hero number and two-line sub-label.
symbols.description: It visualizes a slide's central numeric claim and is normally preceded by a gold triangle pointer. Only one should appear per slide.

symbols.does: Horizontal left-to-right sequence of rounded-rect process nodes linked by gold arrows.
symbols.description: Active nodes receive gold fill and satellite labels describe inputs and outputs. It is used for sales lifecycles, epoch sequences, and competitive positioning, with a recommended capacity of five to eight nodes.

symbols.does: Two equal-width side-by-side panels each carrying a gold section head, bullet list, and metric card row.
symbols.description: Panels are kept at equal height through whitespace padding and may have thin gold borders. It is the standard layout for binary comparisons.

symbols.does: Freeform node-edge graph showing connection through a central hub.
symbols.description: Large central polygonal nodes link to peripheral circles sized by importance, using dashed gold lines for reach and solid gray lines for peer connections. It floats on the page without a frame.

symbols.does: Dashed gold rounded rectangle that groups multiple elements into a notional scope.
symbols.description: It signals a tier, category, or temporal boundary rather than a discrete item. It must always enclose two or more items.

symbols.does: Image tile overlaid with a centered QR code and a Virtual Tour button.
symbols.description: It pairs a proof screenshot with a scannable interactive link. The canonical deployment is a four-column grid.

symbols.does: Full-width bottom band with warm cream and faint diagonal hatch containing concluding text.
symbols.description: It signals a slide-level therefore after complex evidence. It should be used selectively and avoided on slides that are themselves a single thesis.

symbols.does: Scatter-style chart with time on the horizontal axis and value tier on the vertical axis.
symbols.description: Client cards are placed at their current coordinate inside dashed scope boxes, while far-future states are shown at reduced opacity. A gold gradient axis arrow encodes automation or progression.

symbols.does: Full-width horizontal band comparing competitor category columns.
symbols.description: Each column contains a gold category head, bullet attributes, and a logo row, all framed by a dashed-scope rectangle. A V-mark or callout sits outside the frame as the resolution.

symbols.does: Horizontal personnel record combining a circular photo, title, name, year, arrow, and credential text.
symbols.description: It may include an organization logo at the far right. The block must keep its photo-circle, three-tier text hierarchy, and arrow connector as one atomic unit.

symbols.does: Circular placeholder icon with a person silhouette and a centered gold category label.
symbols.description: It is used when an advisory individual is not yet named. The label is allowed to wrap to two lines.

symbols.does: Rounded-rectangle card with gold stroke and cream fill containing a feature label and centered descriptor.
symbols.description: It is used in four-column rows, process spine labels, and any named-item-with-description pattern. Internal padding should remain equal on all sides.

symbols.does: Full-width horizontal band with diagonal-stripe texture holding several stat figures.
symbols.description: Each figure pairs a large gold number with a dark centered descriptor. Only one band should appear per slide, and the texture must not be replaced by a solid tint.

symbols.does: Isolated large gold figure with a centered descriptor below it.
symbols.description: It may live inside a card or in a stat band. Multiple figures compose into horizontal rows for comparative data, aligned by the bottom of the figure to the descriptor baseline.

symbols.does: Stroked circle containing a directional arrow indicating positive or negative trend.
symbols.description: Green stroke signals positive movement and red or terra-cotta signals negative. It should be paired as exactly two circles per slide when showing opposing market forces.

symbols.does: Circle-masked image with a gold-bordered pill button below it.
symbols.description: Video thumbnails may add a play-button overlay. The circle and button form one inseparable unit, typically composed in grids of three or six.

symbols.does: Semi-transparent white rectangle placed bottom-left on a full-bleed photo slide.
symbols.description: It holds a logo and label-value contact rows. It should only be used on warm-toned architectural close slides.

symbols.does: Vertical list where each item is led by a right-pointing gold triangle.
symbols.description: Text is dark, normal-weight, and may be arranged in two columns for parallel information sets. It replaces standard disc bullets and dashes.

symbols.does: Vertical sequence of stage cards connected by a solid spine and dashed branches to images.
symbols.description: Category labels, revision labels, and stage cards run down the left while associated images sit on the right. The solid-versus-dashed grammar carries the semantic structure.

symbols.does: Circular metric container with a large number and small label, rendered with a radial gradient.
symbols.description: It has no stroke and a faint drop shadow, deployed in a horizontal row of three to nine across the full content width. It should never be stacked vertically.

symbols.does: Vertical list prefixed by golden right-pointing triangles.
symbols.description: Text is small grey body with optional inline bold for key terms. It is intended to replace all standard bullet lists, with a maximum of five items and one to two lines per item.

symbols.does: Rectangular card with warm-grey border, white fill, soft shadow, and centered heading.
symbols.description: It may include subtext, a logo strip, or body bullets. It is the universal content container, used in rows of two or four, and the heading must stay on one line.

symbols.does: Regular flat-top hexagon with golden stroke and white fill containing a short concept name.
symbols.description: Large and small sizes label prominent product concepts or card contexts. It is reserved for named system-level concepts such as Virtual Tours, Property Wizard, User Portal, and Vi.

symbols.does: Small bordered rectangle showing a large figure above a small grey label.
symbols.description: It always appears in pairs, aligned to the left edge of a text block or centered under a category heading. It is never used solo or three-in-a-row.

symbols.does: Full-width dashed horizontal line with a centered text label.
symbols.description: It labels content zones within a single slide. Every use must carry a label, and it should not be used as a plain horizontal rule.

symbols.does: Outlined button with transparent fill and single-line medium text.
symbols.description: It uses golden or dark borders with generous padding. It is the standard CTA style and should never be filled.

symbols.does: Circular node arrangement connected by golden lines with arrowheads.
symbols.description: It is used in before-and-after pairs separated by a golden transition arrow, showing full-mesh chaos versus ring or hub-and-spoke order. Node labels must be short.

symbols.does: Team member card with circular photo crop and text block.
symbols.description: The card has a warm-grey border and white fill, with name in golden bold, role, and qualification in small grey. It is always arranged in a two-column grid.

symbols.does: Product screenshot inside a consistent laptop or tablet frame at a slight perspective tilt.
symbols.description: All device frames on one slide share the same tilt angle. Flat and perspective views should not be mixed on the same slide.

symbols.does: Rounded rectangle with golden border and cream fill containing a large bold golden number.
symbols.description: The descriptor label sits outside the box to the right. It is used to emphasize a data figure, always as a box-number plus adjacent-label pair.

symbols.does: Vertical list of single-line items prefixed by a small golden triangle.
symbols.description: Text is regular-weight warm near-black. It is the most repeated component and should be implemented with the marker as a separate element for precise alignment.

symbols.does: Horizontal strip of rounded pill buttons with golden borders and cream fill.
symbols.description: A small icon and golden arrow prefix the row. It is used as a thesis seal at the bottom of product or strategy slides, and pills must never wrap to two lines.

symbols.does: Icon row inside a dashed rounded rectangle with a vertical dashed line down to a circular hub.
symbols.description: The dashed border on the input container contrasts with the solid border on the hub circle. It represents multiple data types feeding into a single platform, always with inputs above and hub below.

symbols.does: Vertical hierarchy with a hub circle at top branching to smaller avatar circles below.
symbols.description: Dashed lines with downward arrows suggest the system distributes to each user type. The number of leaf avatar circles represents the number of user roles.

symbols.does: Horizontal person block inside a light grey rounded container with a circular photo avatar.
symbols.description: It pairs a golden name, dark grey title, and smaller grey role description. The avatar circle uses a real headshot, distinct from diagram avatar circles that use icons.

symbols.does: Pair of branded badges: a hexagon for Property Wizard and a diamond for Vi.
symbols.description: They are connected by a dashed line and appear together whenever the combined system is shown. The shapes are brand-identity-critical and must not be used for other concepts.

symbols.does: Full-width horizontal band with cream tint and faint diagonal stripe texture holding six number-label pairs.
symbols.description: Each pair combines a large golden number with a small dark label. The equal distribution creates a scoreboard rhythm; pairs should not be clustered or unevenly spaced.

symbols.does: Two-column layout with matching headers, screenshot blocks, and bullet lists.
symbols.description: A directional arrow between columns indicates transformation. Columns must be structurally identical, including screenshot dimensions and bullet count.

symbols.does: Small filled golden circle containing a white V lettermark in the bottom-right corner.
symbols.description: It is the brand ownership stamp and must appear on every content slide without exception, resizing, or repositioning.

symbols.does: White rounded-rect overlay panel on an immersive-mode closing slide.
symbols.description: It contains a brand logo and stacked label-value contact pairs. It is the only content element on an immersive slide and is positioned top-left.

symbols.does: Central golden hexagon with radiating circle nodes connected by bidirectional arrows.
symbols.description: The hex center always represents the brand or system, while circle nodes represent categories or parties. A two-line italic caption sits below.

symbols.does: Horizontal band with subtle tinted background holding three to six stat cells.
symbols.description: Each cell places a large bold golden number beside a smaller descriptor on the same line. It is always horizontal and never stacked vertically.

symbols.does: Two equal-width frames side by side with golden subheadings, visuals, and captions.
symbols.description: A horizontal golden arrow centered vertically between the panels signals transformation rather than simple juxtaposition.

symbols.does: Horizontal row of line-art property-stage icons of equal spacing and size.
symbols.description: It may be enclosed by an optional dashed border with a centered italic label below. The set must always deploy as a complete set to signal industry breadth.

symbols.does: Circular portrait with name, role, company label, and optional credibility metrics.
symbols.description: Name is golden bold, role is charcoal, and metrics pair golden bold numbers with charcoal descriptors. It may sit inside a subtle tinted rounded box.

symbols.does: Grid of rounded-rectangle tags with golden borders and no fill.
symbols.description: Tags enumerate parallel concepts such as GTM pillars or property types. They are a visual grammar for peers and should not be used for single items or hierarchy.

symbols.does: Full-width frosted bar containing golden-bordered pill buttons with icons and labels.
symbols.description: It sits at the bottom of a photo-background zone. It is intended only for photo-background contexts, while light-mode slides use individual buttons.

symbols.does: Vertical time axis with milestone boxes connected by L-shaped dashed golden arrows.
symbols.description: Later milestones appear lower and further right, creating a cascade of sequential dependencies. The vertical axis must be labeled with time units, and connectors should never be diagonal.

symbols.does: Full-width bottom strip of alternating golden and white diagonal stripes.
symbols.description: It is a mandatory footer element on every non-photo slide, visually quoting the floor texture from the cover photo render.

symbols.does: Warm-cream rounded rectangle with a large gold bold stat and a shorter descriptor below.
symbols.description: It uses a soft drop shadow and equal padding, deployed in two-up or three-up rows. Content budget is short: one to eight characters for the stat and three to eight words for the descriptor.

symbols.does: Horizontal row of rounded-rectangle tabs with cream fill and gold border.
symbols.description: The active tab takes a gold-wash fill and may carry a star marker. A dashed horizontal rule separates the row from body content. Tab labels must stay on one line.

symbols.does: Circle nodes connected by directional straight-line edges in before and after states.
symbols.description: The before state shows many-to-many dark-brown heavy edges, while the after state places a hexagonal hub at the center with simplified gold ring edges. A filled-triangle arrow marks the transition.

symbols.does: Horizontal sequence of icon-nodes connected by dashed gold arrows in the footer hatch zone.
symbols.description: It represents a linear left-to-right temporal flow such as a user journey. It should contain three to five nodes maximum.

symbols.does: Two or three equal-width cream cards side by side with gold headings and percentage stats.
symbols.description: Columns must be equal-width and equal-height with the same number of data rows. Each row pairs a gold percentage stat with a body descriptor.

symbols.does: Fifty-fifty left-right split showing a before diagram and an after diagram.
symbols.description: A centered filled-triangle transition arrow sits at the vertical midpoint. The before state uses a brown or dark palette and the after state uses gold.

symbols.does: Full-width bottom-pinned band with diagonal hatch texture and a single takeaway.
symbols.description: It contains an icon, a gold number, and a dark descriptor. It is the bottom-most element on data-heavy slides and should hold only one stat or sentence.

symbols.does: Four-quadrant template for vertical market sections.
symbols.description: It combines a SAM box, B2B flow diagram, before/after workflow, and a hatch footer with a market stat. Structural sameness across verticals signals system scalability.

symbols.does: Left-to-right branching tree for hierarchical pricing or tiering.
symbols.description: The root is always a person icon, trunk nodes show model types, branch nodes show tier names, and leaf nodes show price labels in grey. It should not exceed three nesting levels.

symbols.does: Horizontal card with circular portrait, role, golden name, arrow, and credential text.
symbols.description: It is used for team bios or advisor listings. The name is always golden, the role appears above the name, and the credential line is short.

symbols.does: Full-bleed photo overlay with dark tint, large headline, and centered pill toggle or scroll nudge.
symbols.description: It is used on all three landing pages with audience-swapped headline and subtitle while sharing the same photo. A V-badge or wordmark appears in the nav band above.

symbols.does: White rounded card with a line-art icon and a single bold verb label.
symbols.description: It sits in a three-column two-row grid of six tiles. Labels are limited to one of six verbs: Visualise, Inform, Communicate, Collaborate, Market, Sell.

symbols.does: Typographic pair of a large gold bold stat above a two-line dark bold label.
symbols.description: It has no border, card, or background. It is always used in groups of exactly four, appearing twice per parent page for client outcome and company credibility stats.

symbols.does: Rounded pill button with two border variants for hero and mid-page contexts.
symbols.description: It is the universal interactive element, with text limited to one to four words and medium weight. Every clickable action in the system is a pill.

symbols.does: Four-column row of rounded cards showing stage headings, body text, and durations.
symbols.description: One card per set receives a gold border highlight and a downward triangle pointer. The highlighted card changes by use case while the structure remains invariant.

symbols.does: Comparison table with feature names and three project columns.
symbols.description: Project 1 is enclosed in a gold rectangle border, prices are golden bold, and Project 3 uses narrative prose instead of checkmarks. The structure is identical across landing pages.

symbols.does: Alternating left-right layout pairing an icon box with a text block.
symbols.description: Each step shows a gold-bordered rounded box containing a line-art icon, a one-word gold heading, a short paragraph, and a gold-chevron bullet list. The three steps are Upload, Explore, Review.

symbols.does: Narrow full-width strip with diagonal gold-stripe background and two pill buttons.
symbols.description: It prompts audience selection between Architects and Developers. It appears twice on the main landing page and never on deep audience pages.

symbols.does: Closing section with a heading, subtitle, and three to four equal-weight pill buttons.
symbols.description: It offers actions such as Request a Demo, Get a Quote, Make an Enquiry, and Download Info Pack. It is used identically on both audience landing pages.

symbols.does: Near-black footer with three columns for heading/email, phone, and a CTA pill button.
symbols.description: A centered copyright sits at the bottom and a gold V-badge may appear in some variants. CTA text differs by page.

symbols.does: Brand wordmark combining bold black Concept with a custom gold V glyph.
symbols.description: It is centered in the main landing nav or top-right on one audience page. The custom V glyph cannot be substituted with a font character.

symbols.does: Two-column layout contrasting a disordered-file screenshot with a clean product screenshot.
symbols.description: A dashed gold arrow connects the problem side to the solution side. It is used only in the one-pager to compress the argument into six bullets and one visual comparison.

symbols.does: Horizontal connected chevron sequence showing journey stages.
symbols.description: The active stage uses solid amber fill and bold label, while inactive stages use amber outline and grey label. It is used at the top of every stage section and can compress from four to three stages.

symbols.does: Fifty-fifty horizontal split with brand narrative on the left and atmospheric photo on the right.
symbols.description: The right column bleeds to the page edge and includes a bottom scrim with white caption text. It serves as the cover or first-page component.

symbols.does: Full journey stage block with chevron bar, framing paragraph, bullet list, and italic promise line.
symbols.description: It is the repeating unit per journey stage and normally occupies the left half of a split-layout page. Parameters include stage name, frame paragraph, bullets, and promise line.

symbols.does: Two or three architectural or product photos stacked vertically with a small gap.
symbols.description: Gold curved connector arrows may point to float labels adjacent to elements in screenshots. It is the right-column companion to StageSection.

symbols.does: Amber-bordered box containing an institution logo, large stat, attribution, and source citation.
symbols.description: It is intended for professional or skeptical audiences and is used once per deck. The box spans roughly the full content column.

symbols.does: Grid table comparing competitor types against capability attributes.
symbols.description: The ConceptV row is highlighted with a light amber tint and all-amber checkmarks, while competitor rows use red X or amber check. It is unique to the architects deck.

symbols.does: Closing page zone with an amber aspirational headline and two columns.
symbols.description: The left column holds a QR code, outline CTA button, and instruction text, while the right column lists contact label-value pairs. A brand mark sits in the footer.

symbols.does: Small amber-bordered box promising deferred content in compressed decks.
symbols.description: It contains a bold What's next? label and a subtitle describing a coming product or feature. It is used at section end in two-page compressed decks.

symbols.does: Amber-bordered box with a large gold opening quote, italic quote body, and bold right-aligned attribution.
symbols.description: It may contain one or two quotes and is used once per deck near the closing section.

symbols.does: Small rounded rectangle card containing a large gold stat over a grey caption.
symbols.description: It is the atomic stat display unit, used in rows of two, three, or four. The card has off-white fill, a grey or gold border, and fixed approximate dimensions.

symbols.does: Horizontal process flow of five to seven rounded-rectangle nodes connected by right-arrows.
symbols.description: Optional up or down gold arrowhead feeders sit above or below nodes, and one node is rendered as a gold-filled octagon. Dashed connectors indicate projected or optional paths.

symbols.does: Force-directed network of colored circles with thin grey lines inside a dashed-gold border card.
symbols.description: Hub nodes are slightly larger with labels, and gold dashed directional arrows run from hub to cluster. It communicates the reach of the product across a network.

symbols.does: Full-width or half-width zone with a gold section head, arrow-bullet items, and optional stat chips.
symbols.description: It groups content without a visible container, relying on spacing and a light off-white background. It is used for content grouping in two-column layouts.

symbols.does: Horizontal chain of three nodes showing commercial value flow.
symbols.description: It starts with a gold V-logo circle, leads to a bold customer box, then a hollow end-user box, with a dollar-circle node connecting upward to the customer box. It is used side-by-side for Architects and Developers.

symbols.does: Horizontal personnel card with circular photo, role, golden name, year, arrow, and credential text.
symbols.description: It is used for core team and industry personnel. The structure is identical for real people; a silhouette icon can substitute for advisors or placeholders.

symbols.does: White or off-white card with client logos, category label, dollar range, and descriptor.
symbols.description: Cards are constrained to equal width and height in a row. It is used to present proposal tiers or packages.

symbols.does: Scatter chart with time phases on the x-axis and value tiers on the y-axis.
symbols.description: Client logo cards are positioned as nodes along a gold Automation baseline, connected by dashed gold step-paths. Diagonal arrows at the top-right indicate revenue or volume vectors.

symbols.does: Rounded-rectangle card containing a bar chart with a gold category title.
symbols.description: Bars use yellow for growth metrics or orange for cash and conversion metrics. Data labels sit above bars and axis labels are small and grey.

symbols.does: Full-width horizontal band with fine diagonal hatch texture on cream background holding stat cells.
symbols.description: It is the primary macro-data display component, used for rows of three to four market statistics. The hatched background differentiates it from adjacent card grids.

symbols.does: Rounded rectangle with gold border and cream fill containing a centered heading and body text.
symbols.description: It is the core content-zone unit for two to four parallel concepts. It should not be mixed with un-bordered text zones on the same slide.

symbols.does: Quadrant card with octagon badge, horizontal product-name pill, status dot with year, and body paragraph.
symbols.description: It is used for product or pillar definitions. The octagon-plus-pill header row is distinctive and not interchangeable with a plain section head.

symbols.does: Perfect circle photo-fill with an optional gold border and a labeled button below.
symbols.description: A play button overlay may appear for video. The circle and button are atomic and are used in three-by-two or five-by-one grids.

symbols.does: Frosted-glass card with circular portrait, gold title, bold name, and expertise sentence.
symbols.description: Cards are equal-width in a three-column row. The circular portrait is the mandatory first element.

symbols.does: White semi-transparent rectangle anchored to the lower-left of a full-bleed photo slide.
symbols.description: It contains the brand wordmark and contact label-value pairs. It is intended only for closing or contact slides with warm, complex photos.

symbols.code_id: WorkflowStageConnector (stage box + dashed arrow + photo output)
symbols.does: Vertical sequence of stage labels, dashed lines, stage boxes, and right-pointing dashed arrows to photo thumbnails.
symbols.description: Photos increase in visual quality as stages progress, making the progression load-bearing. It is used for process or workflow slides showing staged output improvement.

symbols.does: Compositional zone with project name, metric bullets, photo thumbnail with QR overlay, and partner logos.
symbols.description: Two zones fit on one slide. The QR code must be a live link, and a Virtual Tour pill label appears on the photo.

symbols.does: Full-width bottom strip with light cream or hatched-cream background and a centered conclusion sentence.
symbols.description: It conveys the so what of a slide and should be reserved for high-stakes conclusions, not used on every slide.

symbols.does: Horizontal section header with hexagon badge, gold-bordered title rectangle, and optional outline CTA.
symbols.description: It introduces Virtual Tours, Property Wizard, User Portal, and Vi panels. The hexagon label and section title always appear together.

symbols.does: Horizontal card with circular photo, role, golden name, year, arrow, and expertise text.
symbols.description: It is arranged in two or three columns. The photo-plus-text layout and arrow separator are fixed across team slides.

symbols.does: Row of two, three, or four equal-width gold-bordered cards with centered titles and body text.
symbols.description: It is used for principles, partnerships, and market sections. Card count must remain consistent within a row.

symbols.does: Small gold-bordered card with a large bold gold number and a small grey label.
symbols.description: It is used in pairs or fours within market or entry slides, typically at the bottom of a two-column section as a quantitative summary.

symbols.does: Exactly fifty-fifty horizontal split with gold heading, arrow-bullet list, and optional stat cards in each half.
symbols.description: It is the standard layout for market, acquisition, and problem-comparison slides, with a gutter of about twenty-eight pixels.

symbols.does: Full-width horizontal line with milestone dots, stars, month labels, and a rightward arrowhead.
symbols.description: It sits at the bottom of capital raise and roadmap slides. It should carry no more than six to eight milestones.

symbols.does: Centered horizontal band with diagonal gold-line hatch pattern and one to two closing sentences.
symbols.description: It appears at the bottom of every informational slide and functions as the slide contract.

symbols.does: Full-bleed architectural interior render used as a terminal or transitional closing slide.
symbols.description: It either places a semi-transparent white panel with contact details or a logo with forward CTA text. No grid layout is used.

symbols.does: Central gold-bordered circle with brand and radial arrows to verb-label boxes at cardinal points.
symbols.description: Left flanking columns hold lists of stakeholders and files, while the right side shows a three-by-three photo proof grid with category labels. It is a single-use complex component for innovations-in-practice slides.

symbols.does: Panel below the ProductHeaderRow showing a status dot, status label, and body paragraph.
symbols.description: The dot color carries semantic status: green for live and orange for beta. It always follows the ProductHeaderRow.

symbols.does: Full-width bordered row with a gold Timing label, star icon, and urgency sentence.
symbols.description: It is used exclusively on one slide for urgency signaling. It differs from the HatchedSummaryBand by having a gold border and star icon.
